target.title: [codex] save ChatGPT browser file artifacts
target.body:
## Summary

Implements the narrow file-artifact boundary proposed in steipete/oracle#244:

- add `kind: "file"` session artifacts for ChatGPT browser runs
- discover downloadable file anchors from the assistant response and captured answer text/transcript
- restrict authenticated ChatGPT downloads to known file artifact endpoints only:
  - `/backend-api/files/<id>/download|content`
  - `/backend-api/estuary/content?id=file_*`
  - `/backend-api/sandbox/download?path=/mnt/data/...`
- reject non-file ChatGPT backend APIs such as `/backend-api/me` and conversation endpoints before sending cookies
- recover real ChatGPT behavior buttons such as `Download the CSV` when a `sandbox:` link cannot be fetched directly
- include saved files in session artifact metadata and keep the archive guard from archiving before required artifacts are saved

This intentionally does not fetch arbitrary external links or arbitrary ChatGPT `/backend-api/*` URLs from the answer.

## Why

After #169, browser sessions persist transcripts, Deep Research reports, and generated images, but generic ChatGPT-generated files such as ZIPs, wheels, source distributions, CSVs, or PDFs were still stranded in the live ChatGPT conversation. This extends the same artifact layer without adding a new public API.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec vitest run tests/browser/artifacts.test.ts tests/browser/chatgptFiles.test.ts tests/browser/chatgptImages.test.ts tests/browser/sessionRunner.test.ts tests/cli/sessionRunner.test.ts`
  - 68 tests passed
- `pnpm run check`
- `pnpm run build`
- `git diff --check`

## Live proof status

Component-level real browser smoke passed against an existing ChatGPT conversation that had a real generated CSV download button. The sandbox URL intentionally returned 404 first, then the new button fallback clicked the live ChatGPT `Download the CSV` control and saved the file as an Oracle session artifact:

```text
[browser] Found 1 downloadable file link(s) in captured answer text.
[browser] Failed to save downloadable file 1/1: download failed: 404
[browser] Found 1 downloadable file candidate(s).
[browser] Clicked 1 assistant download button(s).
[browser] Saved 1 downloadable file artifact(s).
```

Redacted saved artifact:

```text
~/.oracle/sessions/pr245-component-live-smoke-1780731237320/artifacts/oracle_pr245_file_artifact_smoke_out(7).csv
mimeType: text/csv
sizeBytes: 26
sourceUrl: browser-download
```

Full fresh Oracle E2E proof was attempted after the fix with session `pr245-file-artifact-live-proof-5`. I am **not** counting it as a successful E2E file-artifact proof: ChatGPT returned a text-only `sandbox:/mnt/data/...` link and no real downloadable file/button for that prompt, so the session correctly completed with transcript only. The log now shows the late transcript recovery pass running, but there was still no actual file to save:

```text
[browser] No assistant download buttons found for button fallback.
[browser] Retrying downloadable file collection from saved transcript.
[browser] No assistant download buttons found for button fallback.
```

This means the authenticated URL boundary and real button-download collector are now covered, but a maintainer/Mantis proof with a ChatGPT run that actually creates a fresh file artifact would still be the best final merge proof.

## Notes

- The archive guard now requires detected browser file artifacts to be saved before auto-archiving, matching the existing generated-image safety model.
- The new regression tests explicitly reject `/backend-api/me`, conversation endpoints, unsafe sandbox paths, and non-`file_` estuary IDs.
target.author: pdurlej
